essence: Leads CNC machining teams, oversees production quality, and ensures machines, people, and processes run efficiently.

identity.title: CNC Supervisor
identity.summary: Manages CNC operators, setters, and machinists; coordinates production schedules; ensures quality standards; and maintains efficient machining operations across manufacturing environments.

salary.notes: Supervisors in aerospace, defence, or high‑precision manufacturing can exceed £70k-£85k+.

qualifications: Experience in CNC machining, setting, or programming
Knowledge of tooling, materials, tolerances, and machining processes
Ability to lead teams and coordinate production workflows
Understanding of quality control, safety standards, and lean manufacturing

lifestyle.description: CNC supervisors work in precision engineering firms, aerospace, automotive, and industrial manufacturing. They manage teams, oversee production quality, and ensure machines run efficiently. The role suits individuals who enjoy leadership, technical expertise, and improving manufacturing performance.

habits: Oversees CNC operators, setters, and machinists
Ensures quality standards and tolerances are met
Troubleshoots machining issues and optimises processes
Maintains safety, documentation, and workflow efficiency
